2604.05906.title: Selective Aggregation of Attention Maps Improves Diffusion-Based Visual Interpretation
2604.05906.paper_summary: Numerous studies on text-to-image (T2I) generative models have utilized cross-attention maps to boost application performance and interpret model behavior. However, the distinct characteristics of attention maps from different attention heads remain relatively underexplored. In this study, we show that selectively aggregating cross-attention maps from heads most relevant to a target concept can improve visual interpretability. Compared to the diffusion-based segmentation method DAAM, our approach achieves higher mean IoU scores. We also find that the most relevant heads capture concept-specific features more accurately than the least relevant ones, and that selective aggregation helps diagnose prompt misinterpretations. These findings suggest that attention head selection offers a promising direction for improving the interpretability and controllability of T2I generation.
2604.05906.paper_summary_zh: 許多關於文本到圖像（T2I）生成模型的研究已經利用交叉注意力圖來提升應用性能並解釋模型行為。<lb/>然而，來自不同注意力頭的注意力圖的獨特特徵仍然相對未被深入探討。<lb/>在本研究中，我們顯示從與目標概念最相關的頭部選擇性地聚合交叉注意力圖可以改善視覺可解釋性。<lb/>與基於擴散的分割方法DAAM相比，我們的方法實現了更高的平均IoU分數。<lb/>我們還發現，最相關的頭部比最不相關的頭部更準確地捕捉概念特定的特徵，並且選擇性聚合有助於診斷提示誤解。<lb/>這些發現表明，注意力頭選擇為改善T2I生成的可解釋性和可控性提供了一個有前景的方向。
2604.05906.author: Jungwon Park et.al.
2604.05906.authors: Jungwon Park, Jungmin Ko, Dongnam Byun, Wonjong Rhee
